exercises.name: Alternating Single-Leg Forward Hop
exercises.description: Hop forward on one leg, maintaining control and balance.

exercises.name: Alternating Spiderman lunge to hip lift
exercises.description: Start in a plank position, bringing one foot towards your hand in a Spiderman stretch, then transition into a hip lift. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Amanda Latona's Front & Side Lateral Raises | Deltoid Exercise #2
exercises.description: Amanda Latona's Front & Side Lateral Raises | Deltoid Exercise #2

exercises.name: AMAZING Exercise For His Shoulder Pain!
exercises.description: AMAZING Exercise For His Shoulder Pain!

exercises.name: American Kettlebell Single Arm Swing
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you want to ensure back and shoulder safety. When at the bottom of the swing, have a slight arch to your back. 

2.When you’re at the top of the swing, ensure a tight chest and shoulder and keep your arm locked tight to your head. 

3. Place the Kettlebell between your feet. Push back with your butt, bend your knees and look straight ahead to get into the starting position.

4 Swing the Kettlebell between your legs forcefully. Quickly reverse the direction and drive though with your hips taking the Kettlebell straight up. 
5.Let the Kettlebell swing back between your legs and repeat. 

6.When you complete one side, do the other.

exercises.name: American Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you want to ensure back safety. Drive from your hips and lower body, follow through with your shoulders. 

2.When the kettlebell is overhead, keep your core tight and locked out your chest and shoulders.

3. Place the kettlebell between your feet. Push back with your butt, bend your knees and look straight ahead to get into the starting position.

4 Swing the kettlebell between your legs forcefully. Quickly reverse the direction and drive though with your hips taking the kettlebell straight up.
 
5.Let the kettlebell swing back between your legs and repeat.

exercises.name: Anchored Reverse Crunch
exercises.description: Lie on your back and anchor your legs, then perform a reverse crunch, lifting your hips towards your chest.

exercises.name: Anchored Straight Leg Raises
exercises.description: Anchor your legs and perform straight leg raises, engaging the core to lift your legs towards the ceiling and slowly lowering them back down.

exercises.name: Angled Machine Leg Press
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the leg press machine, with feet shoulder-width apart on the plate. 

2. Slowly lower the weight towards your body, while keeping your lower back in the seat, as low as you can comfortably and under control. 

3. Push the weight back up to the start position. Avoid completely straightening or "locking out" the knees at the top. Repeat.

exercises.name: Angled Machine Single Leg Press
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the leg press machine, with a single foot on the plate. 

2. Slowly lower the weight towards your body, while keeping your lower back in the seat, as low as you can comfortably and under control. 

3. Push the weight back up to the start position. Avoid completely straightening or "locking out" the knees at the top. Repeat.

exercises.name: Angled Machine Sumo Leg Press
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the leg press machine, with feet placed wider than shoulder-width apart on the plate. 

2. Slowly lower the weight towards your body, while keeping your lower back in the seat, as low as you can comfortably and under control. 

3. Push the weight back up to the start position. Avoid completely straightening or "locking out" the knees at the top. Repeat.

exercises.name: Ankle Alphabets
exercises.description:
1. Standing on one leg, slowly draw the letter "A" with the toes of the leg that is in the air. 

2. Draw the biggest "A" that you can, moving through the biggest range of motion possible. 

3. Continue by drawing the letters "B, C, D" and so on, until you've drawn all the letters of the alphabet.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Ankle Dorsiflexion
exercises.description:
1. Stand on one leg, and hold the opposite foot out in front of you. 

2. Draw the toes of the airborne foot up towards your shin.

3. Relax the toes back down towards the floor, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Ankle Inversion and Eversion
exercises.description:
1. For this mobility and warm-up exercise, feel free to hold on to something for balance.

2. Move your foot sideways all the way out to the side and then inside as far as you can.

3. Switch between the two. Try to get as much range as you can.

4. Once you’re done one side, switch and do the other.

exercises.name: Ankle Plantar and Dorsiflexion
exercises.description:
1.For this warm up, feel free to hold on to something for balance.

2.You'll end up pointing your toe and then bringing your toe all the way back towards your knee.

3.Moving that back and forth between the 2 positions until your ankle and calf feel warmed up.

4.Once you're done 1 side, go ahead and switch to the other.

exercises.name: Ankle Plantarflexion
exercises.description:
1. Stand on one leg, and hold the opposite foot out in front of you. 

2. Push the toes of the airborne leg foot towards the floor. 

3. Relax the toes back up towards the shin, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Ape Walk
exercises.description:
1) Start in a position with your knuckles (or hands) placed on the ground, hips high in the air. One side of your body should have your arm and leg close together, the other side should have your arm and leg further apart from one another. Keep your knees straightened throughout the duration of the exercise. 

2) Keep your body and hips high while staying as square to the ground with your hips and shoulders as possible. 

3) Next, simultaneously reach as far forward as you can with the hand that is furthest back (the one touching your knee), and step forward with the foot furthest back (the one stretched out). Keep your core engaged but move primarily from your abs/obliques.  

4) Repeat alternating sides until provided reps or distances are completed.

exercises.name: Arm Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this warm-up, start with a small range motion. 

2) Slowly build your way to a larger circle. 

3) Go back the other direction.

exercises.name: Arms Overhead Reverse Lunge to Side Bend
exercises.description: Perform a reverse lunge while keeping your arms overhead, then bend sideways toward the front leg for a full-body stretch.

exercises.name: Around the World
exercises.description:
1.Holding the PVC pipe or wooden dowel in a wide grip position, stand with feet about shoulder width apart, body in an upright position.

2.Twist around your body. Make sure to keep straight elbows.

3.Reverse the movement.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Around The World Lunges
exercises.description:
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and dumbbells at your sides (optional). Step your right leg forward into a lunge, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your torso upright. 

Push back to the starting position. Next, step the same leg out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the working leg while keeping the other leg straight. Push back to the center. 

Then step the same leg backward into a reverse lunge, maintaining balance and control. Return to standing. That completes one full sequence.

exercises.name: Assisted Leg Lowering to Bolster
exercises.description:
1. Lie on your back, holding a band looped around the middle of one foot. 

2. Draw both of your legs directly up into the air, with the knees as straight as possible. 

3. Slowly lower the leg without the band down to touch your heel to a foam roller on the floor. 

4. Return to the start position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Assisted pull up machine wide grip
Away Facing Cable Pulldown
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright on a bench, grasp handles from a high pulley, arms and shoulders extended.

2.Pull down the cable, elbows towards the ribs. Pause.

3.Raise the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: B Stance Smith Squat
exercises.description: B Stance Smith Squat

exercises.name: Backward Sled Drag
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a slight extension in through the spine.

2.) From there, you’re going to reach backwards as far as you can with your feet as you step backwards.

exercises.name: Backward/forward glute shuffle
exercises.description: The Frankenstein steps are intentional. Take small steps and keep weight centered so that you can easily step forward or back.

exercises.name: Balance Board Elbow Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to place you balance point, the point where you are going to have most of your body weight into the center of the board.

2) From there, lift your body up, glutes on, abdominals engaged to keep nice straight line throughout your body.

3) Raise your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and feet. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your elbows.

4) Hold for the prescribed duration of time, or until your form is lost.

5) When you’ve completed one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Balance Board Full Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your hands are placed equal distances from the center of the balance board.

2) Keep your abdominals engaged and glutes active to keep neutral spine throughout the exercise.

3) Place your hands on the balance board. Extend your arms until they are straight, and center the board to balance with all sides off of the ground.

4) Raise your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and feet. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands.

5) Hold for the prescribed duration of time, or until your form is lost."

exercises.name: Balance Board Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to lift your hips by engaging your glutes. You’ll also want your abdominal to be active in order to maintain a steady balance board and a quiet body.

2) Lie on your back with your feet flat on a balance board (the wider apart the easier), knees bent, arms at your sides, and abs engaged. 

3) Push your hips toward the ceiling, lifting from your glutes. 

4) Slowly lower your hips back down to the start position. 

5) Repeat until prescribed reps, or holds are completed.

exercises.name: Balance Board Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals and glutes are engaged to maintain neutral spine throughout the push up.

2) You’ll also want to try to place your hand wide enough that they stay underneath your elbows at the bottom of the push up.

3) Sometimes, the balance board might not be long enough.

4) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Gradually put an equal amount of body weight over your hands as you extend your legs out behind you one at a time.

5) Move your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and ankles. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands. 

6) Once in the top of a push up position, lower yourself to the balance board while maintaining neutral spine. Keep your elbows over top of your hands, and in line with your chest. 

7) Repeat as prescribed.

exercises.name: Balance Board Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, line up your hand directly underneath your shoulder that should be placed at the center of the balance board.

2) As you lift up, engage your glutes and have your abdominal nice and active.

3) Lie on your side and place your elbow and forearm centered on top of the balance board. 

4) Stack your feet on top of each other, or lay them next to each other (one in front of the other). 

5) Lift your hips, squeezing your glutes, extending your legs, and drawing abs in (engaging them). Ensure your shoulder is over top of your elbow. Hold this neutral spine position.

6) When you’ve completed one side, repeat it on the other.
